title: raoulia hectorii
intro: Raoulia hectorii, also known as the Hector's pumpkin or Raoulia, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family. It is native to New Zealand and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, Raoulia hectorii is often combined with other plants to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
